findings.issue: README.md build handoff document is generated as part of Phase 10 but the actual content presented to founder shows it as already complete, creating false impression of deliverable status.
findings.evidence: Phase template states "Generate the Build Handoff Document (README.md)" as a Phase 10 output in conversation area 5, but the deliverable review shows fully polished, multi-section README already provided to founder with specific Ocean Golf content, course names, and exact pricing ($75-150/person).
findings.recommendation: Clarify in README whether this is a template-generated first draft requiring founder review/approval, or a final deliverable ready for repository push; if final, confirm founder explicitly approved content in Phase 10 conversation.

findings.issue: Template specifies repository structure but README.md does not declare which exact folder structure was used during push, creating ambiguity for build tools about where to find deliverables.
findings.evidence: Phase template defines 7-folder structure (specifications/, build/, operations/, legal/, manifests/, validation/, reference/) but README provides only high-level folder descriptions without confirming the exact path structure used during the Phase 10 push or verifying files were actually pushed to those locations.
findings.recommendation: Add section to README documenting actual folder paths used (e.g., "Your 20 deliverables are organized in these folders: specifications/d01-platform-vision.md, specifications/d02-user-personas.md...") and confirm push completion for each folder.

findings.issue: Phase 10 template requires pricing verification gate (D-61) with live service pricing checks executed immediately before push, but deliverable shows no evidence that this verification was actually performed for Ocean Golf's 9 specified services.
findings.evidence: Template states "Query pattern: Multiple queries, grouped by category (5+ services per query)...Expected output: Current price for every service in D10. Compare each against manifest value." But README and Build Handoff document contain no timestamp, no research log, no price verification results, and no statement like "All pricing verified as of [exact timestamp]."
findings.recommendation: Add explicit "Pricing Verification Completed" section to README showing verification timestamp, list of 9 services checked (Neon, Stripe, Clerk, Resend, Railway, Sentry, UptimeRobot, Google Analytics), and confirmation that current prices match D10 manifest values or note any discrepancies corrected.

findings.issue: Phase template requires build tool compatibility check before repository structure planning, but deliverable shows no evidence this research was executed or that build tool version requirements were verified.
findings.evidence: Phase template states "10.1 — Build Tool Compatibility Check (ENRICHMENT): Engine: Perplexity Sonar; Trigger: Before structuring the repository" with expected output "Confirmation that build tools haven't changed." But README contains no research note, no tool version verification, and no statement about Cursor IDE, Claude Code CLI, or GitHub Spec Kit compatibility.
findings.recommendation: Add "Build Tool Compatibility Verification" section documenting which versions of Cursor IDE, Claude Code, and GitHub Spec Kit were verified as compatible with the deliverable format, along with verification timestamp.

findings.issue: Template requires verification that every file was pushed successfully with re-fetch confirmation, but deliverable shows no push verification log or confirmation that all 20+ files actually exist at their target repository locations.
findings.evidence: Template states "For each deliverable: (3) Push to the target location...verify the push: re-fetch, confirm byte size is reasonable, confirm EOF sentinel. (5) Report progress to the founder." But README provides no push log, no file count verification, no "Pushed D1...verified ✓" progress tracking, and no final confirmation that [N]/20 deliverables are live in the repository.
findings.recommendation: Add "Repository Push Verification Log" section documenting each file pushed with status (✓ verified), file size confirmation, and final summary: "All 20 deliverables + manifests + validation report + decision history + README + scaffold spec = [N] files successfully pushed to [repository URL]."

findings.issue: Template requires "Run a final repository verification: list all files in the repository, confirm the count matches expectations" but deliverable provides no file inventory, file count, or total repository size.
findings.evidence: Template completion gate requires "Repository file count matches expected total" and phase conversation area 6 specifies "Present the completed package to the founder: Repository URL, File count and total size, Pricing freshness timestamp, Phase 9 certification reference." But README lists no file count, no total size, and no repository URL.
findings.recommendation: Add "Repository Inventory" table listing all pushed files with counts by category (20 deliverables, N manifest files, 1 validation report, 1 decision history, 1 README, 1 scaffold spec = total X files, Y GB) and confirm repository URL where founder can access the package.

findings.issue: README references specific Ocean Golf data (course names, pricing, features, user roles, build cards) but does not declare when this content was finalized or whether founder approved the final version before push.
findings.evidence: README includes specific Ocean Golf implementation details: "14 courses in Cabo," "$75–150 service fee," "12 MVP features," "120 build cards (FC-001 through FC-120)," detailed feature list (F-001 Booking Form, F-002 Rafael's Dashboard, etc.). These match prior deliverables (D1-D20) but README does not state "Founder approved final README on [date]" or "This README was generated from validated deliverables D1-D20 on [date]."
findings.recommendation: Add header to README: "Build Handoff Document — Generated from Phase 9 Validated Deliverables (D1-D20) | Phase 10 Push Date: [date] | Founder Approval: ✅ [name], [date]" to establish chain of custody and approval timestamp.

findings.issue: README reading order section recommends "Before you start building (30 minutes): 1. This document... 2. D9 (Credential Guide)... 3. D10 (Cost Model)" but does not explain why founder should not read D1 (Platform Vision) first, which would provide business context.
findings.evidence: README states founder should read D9 and D10 before D1, but founder has not built the platform yet and lacks business context about competitive positioning, revenue model, and operational constraints that D1 establishes. Phase template specifies reading order for build tools, not founder.
findings.recommendation: Clarify reading order: suggest founder reads D1 first (30 min) for business context, then D9/D10 for implementation planning, rather than jumping to credentials without understanding the strategic intent.

findings.section: How to Start Building - Step 1 (Credential Setup)
findings.issue: README directs founder to D9 credential guide but does not explain the time investment or provide a completion checklist, creating potential for founder to abandon setup mid-process.
findings.evidence: README states "Open operations/d09-credential-guide.md. Complete the 'Pre-Build' section before doing anything else." But provides no checklist of the 12 accounts required, no time estimate (template specifies "2-3 hours total" but README omits this), and no validation step to confirm all credentials are actually saved.
findings.recommendation: Add quick summary: "D9 credential setup requires 2-3 hours and involves creating 12 accounts (GitHub, Supabase, Stripe, Resend, Clerk, Railway, Sentry, UptimeRobot, Google Analytics, domain registrar, password manager, hosting provider). Use the checklist in D9 to confirm each account is created before moving to Step 2."

findings.section: How to Start Building - Step 3 (Run Build Cards)
findings.issue: README instructs founder to "Run build cards in order" but does not address what to do if a build card fails, creating risk of blocking progress without clear recovery path.
findings.evidence: README states "Run them in order. Commit after each one. If a card fails three times, skip it and move to the next independent card — then come back to it." But does not specify how to identify "independent" cards or how to recover from skipped cards without introducing technical debt or breaking downstream cards.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit guidance: "Build cards have dependencies documented in D8. If a card fails, check Section 3 of D8 to see which other cards depend on it. If safe to skip, do so and return after completing independent cards. If other cards depend on it, pause and get help from AI tool (paste the error message and card ID)."

findings.section: Recommended Reading Order - Before Launch Section
findings.issue: README recommends founder send D12 (Privacy Policy) and D13 (Terms of Service) to lawyer "before launch" but does not provide timeline or cost estimate, creating risk of legal documents not being ready by September 1 launch date.
findings.evidence: README states "D12 — Privacy Policy: ⚠️ Have a lawyer review this before publishing. Estimated cost: $500–$1,500. D13 — Terms of Service: ⚠️ Same — lawyer review before publishing." But does not specify when to send them (immediately after Phase 7 build completes on June 10? during Phase 9 pre-launch setup?), how long review takes (2 weeks? 4 weeks?), or what happens if lawyer requires revisions.
findings.recommendation: Clarify timeline: "Send D12 and D13 to lawyer on June 15 (right after Phase 7 build completes). Budget 2-4 weeks for review and revisions. Confirm with lawyer by July 31 at latest (60 days before launch) to allow time for final revisions. Estimated cost: $1,000-$2,000 for both documents."

findings.issue: README lists 11 technology choices with justifications but does not reference which deliverable (D5 Technical Architecture) explains each choice or how to troubleshoot if a tool is not working as expected.
findings.evidence: README provides table with "Layer | Choice | Why" format but does not link each choice back to D5 or provide "if this breaks, see [section]" guidance. For example, "Stripe — Industry standard, test mode for development, global support" lacks pointer to D5 Section 2.3 or to D18 for monitoring Stripe webhook failures.
findings.recommendation: Add reference column to technology stack table: "Tool | Section in D5 | Troubleshooting in D18 | Setup in D9" so founder can quickly find relevant documentation if a tool malfunctions during build.

findings.section: Critical Success Criteria for Phase 7 Build
findings.issue: README lists 8 success criteria (12 MVP features, zero critical bugs, 120 build cards complete, etc.) but does not provide acceptance test procedures or explain how founder will know if each criterion is actually met.
findings.evidence: README states "✅ 12 MVP features fully implemented — F-001 through F-012 are complete and tested" but does not specify what "complete and tested" means. Does it mean feature works in localhost? deployed to production? passes user acceptance testing with Lucia?
findings.recommendation: Add "Acceptance Test" column next to each success criterion: e.g., "12 MVP features: Each feature has a passing test in build card acceptance criteria section (D8); feature has been deployed to staging environment and manually tested by Lucia or Rafael."

findings.issue: README provides generic help guidance but does not reference specific sections of D20 (Maintenance Playbook) that address common build-time issues like deployment failures, database migration errors, or Stripe integration bugs.
findings.evidence: README states "Build tool issues: Start a new conversation in Claude.ai with the specific error message and the build card you're working on" but does not point founder to D20 incident response procedures or troubleshooting checklist that may address the issue more efficiently.
findings.recommendation: Add cross-references: "For common issues, see D20 Troubleshooting section before opening a new conversation: D20 Section 4.2 covers deployment failures, Section 4.3 covers database errors, Section 4.4 covers payment processing failures."

findings.issue: README provides 11-item checklist but does not indicate estimated time to complete all items or provide a target completion date for Phase 7 start on April 15.
findings.evidence: README lists checklist items but concludes with "You're ready" without stating "This checklist should take [X hours] and be completed by [date] to stay on track for April 15 build start." Founder may not understand urgency or timing.
findings.recommendation: Add summary line: "Complete this checklist by April 14 end-of-day to be ready for Phase 7 build start on April 15. Estimated time: 8-12 hours over 1 week. If blocked on any item, escalate immediately."

findings.issue: README metadata table at bottom lists standard fields but omits critical runtime information: Phase 10 pricing verification timestamp, build tool compatibility check timestamp, repository push completion timestamp.
findings.evidence: README shows "Phase 9 Certification: [Date], Phase 10 Pricing Verification: [Date and time]" as examples but deliverable provides no actual timestamps, suggesting Phase 10 verification steps were not executed or not logged.
findings.recommendation: Ensure metadata table includes these three timestamps: Phase 10 Pricing Verification (from D-61 gate), Build Tool Compatibility Check (from research 10.1), Repository Push Completion (from conversation area 4). Add to metadata: "All Pricing Verified: [timestamp] | Build Tool Compatibility: [timestamp] | Repository Push Completed: [timestamp]"

findings.section: Phase 10 Completion Gate - Implicit Missing Section
findings.issue: Template specifies completion gate requirements but README does not include "Methodology Feedback Report" section documenting that Phase 10 observations were compiled and stored separately from founder-facing deliverables.
findings.evidence: Phase template states "Push this report to the project repo root as `methodology-feedback-report.md` — NOT in the founder-facing `artifacts/current/` directory. This is an internal artifact only." But README contains no reference to this internal report or confirmation that it was generated and pushed.
findings.recommendation: Add internal note (not in founder-facing README): "Methodology Feedback Report (methodology-feedback-report.md) has been generated and pushed to repository root. This internal document is not visible in founder's package but available for Phase 10+ process improvement."

findings.section: No timestamp on README document itself
findings.issue: README does not include generation timestamp or Phase 10 push date, making it impossible to verify when the package was completed or if it is stale.
findings.evidence: README header shows "# Ocean Golf — Build Handoff" but no "Generated: [date/time]" or "Pushed to GitHub: [date/time]" field. Phase template specifies "Pushed D21 (Build Handoff README) to repository root as README.md" but no confirmation line appears in the document itself.
findings.recommendation: Add to README top matter: "Generated: April 13, 2026 | Pushed to GitHub: April 13, 2026, 14:30 UTC | Founder Approval: ✅ Rafael Delgado" to establish document currency and approval chain.
